_note: Authored half of the fab card. {tokens} are filled per-instance from numbers(mod); geometry/counts come from the model, never from this file.
title: STANDARD WALL PANEL

procedure.text: Mark stud layout on both plates: {layout}″.
Screw studs through plates (3-⅛″ T25) → frame.
Drill interconnect holes in side studs —
procedure.note: [OSE] 24″ from each end + ctr; L Ø½″, R Ø¼″; 1″ ctr pin.

procedure.text: Staple house wrap; 18″ bottom / 6″ top overhang.
Fasten OSB, ¾″ down from panel top.
Flip; install R-19 batts between studs.
Apply sill gasket to bottom plate.

hole_pattern.label: SIDE-STUD INTERCONNECT DRILL
hole_pattern.fence: OSE TEMPLATE — not in model geometry
notes: Left stud Ø½″ (bolt slips) · Right stud Ø¼″ (bolt bites)
1″ centering-pin hole, 3″ above center · 3× ½″ lag bolts at seam

salient: Clear bays {first_bay}″; last bay {last_bay}″ — end stud sits flush at the {width}″ edge.
Single top plate — a lapping cap plate is added at assembly to tie modules (= double top plate).
Plain panel has no edge blocking; adjacent panels meet at a doubled stud, lag-bolted (see interconnect).
